description_long: This guard rail consists of two horizontal wooden beams supported by two vertical wooden posts, all rendered in a reddish-brown color. The wood texture is visibly rough, with natural grain patterns and slight irregularities, giving it a rustic appearance. The beams are rectangular in cross-section and are mounted parallel to each other, spaced apart to provide a barrier while allowing visibility through the gap. The ends of the beams are blunt and squared off, with no additional end treatments or safety features. There are no visible fasteners, reflective markings, or metal reinforcements, suggesting a simple, utilitarian design. The overall construction appears sturdy and is typical for use in parks, trails, or as a low-impact urban barrier.
description: A rustic, reddish-brown wooden guard rail with two horizontal beams and two vertical posts, featuring a rough wood texture and blunt ends.
description_view_0: Front view showing both horizontal beams and the two supporting vertical posts, highlighting the wood grain and overall structure.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the narrow profile of the guard rail, with the thickness of the beams and posts visible.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, displaying the same two beams and posts, with wood texture details.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the slim profile and the alignment of the beams and posts.
